canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, TimeQA is consistently described as a benchmark for temporal or time-sensitive question answering. It is defined as being derived from natural language and Wikidata, with a focus on "real-world factual questions about event-time relationships" (Binary Classifier Optimization for Large Language Model Alignment). The instrument is used to measure constructs such as `Factuality` and `Mathematical reasoning`. Specifically, its tasks are reported to involve "ordering, arithmetic and co-temporal inference," where it reveals "large gaps between model and human performance" (FANS: Formal Answer Selection forLLMNatural Language Math Reasoning Using Lean4). One paper characterizes it as one of the larger benchmarks for this purpose. It is also used as a source for other evaluation datasets, such as DATEAUGBENCH.
papers: Binary Classifier Optimization for Large Language Model Alignment
Information Locality as an Inductive Bias for Neural Language Models
Investigating and Enhancing the Robustness of Large Multimodal Models Against Temporal Inconsistency
FANS: Formal Answer Selection forLLMNatural Language Math Reasoning Using Lean4
Your Language Model Can Secretly Write Like Humans: Contrastive Paraphrase Attacks onLLM-Generated Text Detectors

definitions.text: A time-sensitive question-answering benchmark derived from natural language and Wikidata, focusing on real-world factual questions about event-time relationships.
definitions.paper: Binary Classifier Optimization for Large Language Model Alignment

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset for temporal question answering, used as a source for the context-based task in DATEAUGBENCH.
definitions.paper: FANS: Formal Answer Selection forLLMNatural Language Math Reasoning Using Lean4
